author: y4rr
content: Decision: Using catch_unwind(|| CubeRuntime::new(CubeDevice::Wgpu(0)).is_ok()) as the probe. Alternatives considered: (1) wgpu::Instance::new + enumerate_adapters + filter by vendor 0x8086 — requires adding wgpu as a direct dep and async executor; (2) CubeRuntime::auto() — returns Some when any adapter available, not Intel-specific; (3) catch_unwind(CubeRuntime::new) — reuses existing imports, no new deps, matches the xpu() helper pattern in tests. Chose option 3: consistent with how hardware absence is already handled, minimal diff, no new deps. Note: CubeRuntime::new on wgpu panics in the worker thread when no Vulkan adapter exists (WSL2 path), so catch_unwind is required. After fix, is_available() → false on this box because CubeRuntime::new panics → catch_unwind returns Err → is_ok() = false.
